Operation 'listWorkspaces' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details, poor_ai_interpretability. Original description is identical to summary and provides no actionable context. Enhanced version clarifies that this lists user-accessible workspaces, specifies return structure, and explains use case for AI agents to understand when to call this operation.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a list of all workspaces accessible to the authenticated user. Returns workspace objects containing workspace identifiers, names, and metadata. This operation is useful for discovering available workspaces before performing workspace-scoped operations.
data.reasoning: Original description is identical to summary and provides no actionable context. Enhanced version clarifies that this lists user-accessible workspaces, specifies return structure, and explains use case for AI agents to understand when to call this operation.

Original description lacks domain context and input/output details. Enhanced version explains what traffic types are in Split.io context, clarifies the required workspaceId parameter, and specifies the response structure for better AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all traffic types defined in the specified workspace. Traffic types represent the entities (e.g., users, accounts, organizations) that can be targeted by feature flags and experiments. Requires the workspace ID as a path parameter. Returns a list of traffic type objects with identifiers and names.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks domain context and input/output details. Enhanced version explains what traffic types are in Split.io context, clarifies the required workspaceId parameter, and specifies the response structure for better AI agent understanding of when and how to use this operation.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves all API keys associated with the specified workspace. API keys are used for authentication with the Split.io Admin API and SDKs. Requires the workspace ID as a path parameter. Returns a list of API key objects containing key identifiers, names, creation dates, and status information.
data.reasoning: Original description is too generic and lacks context about purpose and parameters. message: Operation 'listSegments' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details. Original description is too minimal. Added context about what segments are used for, clarified the response structure, and explained pagination support to help AI agents understand when and how to use this operation.

data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve all segments in an environment
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of all segments defined in the specified workspace and environment. Segments are used to group users for targeting in feature flags. Returns a SegmentList containing segment metadata including name, description, and key count. Supports pagination via offset and limit parameters.
data.reasoning: Original description is too minimal. Added context about what segments are used for, clarified the response structure, and explained pagination support to help AI agents understand when and how to use this operation.

message: Operation 'createSegment' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description lacks essential details. Added information about required request fields, immediate availability, return value, and uniqueness constraint to enable AI agents to properly construct requests and understand the operation's effects.

data.summary_suggestion: Create a new segment in an environment
data.description_suggestion: Creates a new segment in the specified workspace and environment. Requires a CreateSegmentRequest with segment name and description. The segment is immediately available for use in feature flag targeting rules. Returns the created Segment object with its unique identifier and metadata. Segment names must be unique within the environment.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks essential details. Added information about required request fields, immediate availability, return value, and uniqueness constraint to enable AI agents to properly construct requests and understand the operation's effects.

message: Operation 'deleteSegment' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_side_effects. Original description is incomplete. Added context about permanent deletion, impact on feature flags, and irreversibility to help AI agents understand the consequences of this destructive operation.

data.summary_suggestion: Delete a segment from an environment
data.description_suggestion: Deletes a segment from the specified workspace and environment. The segment is permanently removed and can no longer be used in feature flag targeting rules. Any existing feature flags referencing this segment will have their rules updated. This operation cannot be undone.
data.reasoning: Original description is incomplete. Added context about permanent deletion, impact on feature flags, and irreversibility to help AI agents understand the consequences of this destructive operation.

message: Operation 'listSegmentKeys' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details. Original description lacks clarity about what keys represent and the response structure. Added context about keys being user identifiers, clarified pagination support, and explained the return value for better AI agent understanding.

data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve all keys in a segment
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of all keys (user identifiers) currently included in the specified segment. Keys represent individual users or entities targeted by the segment. Returns a SegmentKeyList containing the keys and pagination metadata. Supports pagination via offset and limit parameters.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks clarity about what keys represent and the response structure. Added context about keys being user identifiers, clarified pagination support, and explained the return value for better AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'addSegmentKeys' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description is too brief. Added details about what keys represent, immediate effect on targeting, handling of duplicates, and return value to help AI agents understand the operation's purpose and effects.

data.summary_suggestion: Add user keys to a segment
data.description_suggestion: Adds one or more keys (user identifiers) to the specified segment in the given workspace and environment. Keys are added to the segment's membership list and immediately become eligible for targeting by feature flags using this segment. Duplicate keys are ignored. Returns the updated segment with the new key count.
data.reasoning: Original description is too brief. Added details about what keys represent, immediate effect on targeting, handling of duplicates, and return value to help AI agents understand the operation's purpose and effects.

message: Operation 'removeSegmentKeys' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Original description lacks essential context. Added clarification about what keys represent, the immediate effect on feature flag targeting, handling of non-existent keys, and return value for improved AI agent interpretability.

data.summary_suggestion: Remove user keys from a segment
data.description_suggestion: Removes one or more keys (user identifiers) from the specified segment in the given workspace and environment. Removed keys are no longer eligible for targeting by feature flags using this segment. Non-existent keys are ignored. Returns the updated segment with the new key count.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks essential context. Added clarification about what keys represent, the immediate effect on feature flag targeting, handling of non-existent keys, and return value for improved AI agent interpretability.

message: Operation 'listEnvironments' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, vague_description, missing_context, missing_output_details. Original description is minimal. Added context about what environments represent and their purpose in the Split platform, clarified the response structure, and explained the workspace-environment relationship to help AI agents understand when to use this operation.

data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve all environments in a workspace
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a list of all environments defined in the specified workspace. Environments represent different deployment contexts (e.g., development, staging, production) where feature flags and segments are managed independently. Returns an array of Environment objects containing environment names and metadata.
data.reasoning: Original description is minimal. Added context about what environments represent and their purpose in the Split platform, clarified the response structure, and explained the workspace-environment relationship to help AI agents understand when to use this operation.

data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve all feature flags in workspace
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of all feature flags (splits) within the specified workspace. Supports filtering and sorting via query parameters. Returns a SplitList object containing splits array and pagination metadata. Requires workspace ID and valid authentication.

Original description lacks critical details about request structure, return value, and initial state. Enhanced version specifies request schema expectations, return object, and important side effect that split starts disabled, enabling AI agents to understand the complete operation lifecycle.

data.description_suggestion: Creates a new feature flag (split) in the specified workspace. Accepts a CreateSplitRequest containing split name, traffic type, and initial configuration. Returns the created Split object with generated ID and metadata. The split is created in a disabled state and must be configured per environment before use.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks critical details about request structure, return value, and initial state. Enhanced version specifies request schema expectations, return object, and important side effect that split starts disabled, enabling AI agents to understand the complete operation lifecycle.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves detailed information about a specific feature flag (split) by name within the specified workspace. Returns a Split object containing the split's configuration, metadata, and associated traffic type. Requires valid workspace ID and split name.
data.reasoning: Original description is too minimal. Improved version clarifies what information is returned, specifies the required parameters (workspace ID and split name), and explains the return structure for better AI agent decision-making.

message: Operation 'deleteSplit' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, missing_context. Original description lacks critical information about scope of deletion (all environments), permanence, and response code. Enhanced version clarifies the destructive nature of the operation and its side effects, helping AI agents understand the consequences before executing.

data.description_suggestion: Permanently deletes a feature flag (split) from the specified workspace. This operation removes the split and all its associated definitions across all environments. Requires workspace ID and split name. This action cannot be undone. Returns 204 No Content on successful deletion.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks critical information about scope of deletion (all environments), permanence, and response code. Enhanced version clarifies the destructive nature of the operation and its side effects, helping AI agents understand the consequences before executing.

data.current_summary: Get split definition for an environment
data.current_description: Get split definition for an environment
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the split definition (configuration and rules) for a specific feature flag in a particular environment. Returns a SplitDefinition object containing treatments, traffic allocation, targeting rules, and rollout configuration. Requires workspace ID, split name, and environment name.
data.reasoning: Original description is repetitive and lacks detail about what constitutes a split definition. Improved version specifies the returned SplitDefinition structure and clarifies all required parameters for AI agent understanding.

data.current_summary: Update split definition for an environment
data.current_description: Update split definition for an environment
data.description_suggestion: Updates the split definition (rules, treatments, and traffic allocation) for a feature flag in a specific environment. Accepts a SplitDefinition object with updated configuration. Returns the updated SplitDefinition object. Changes take effect immediately in the specified environment. Requires workspace ID, split name, and environment name.
data.reasoning: Original description lacks specificity about what can be updated and when changes take effect. Enhanced version clarifies the request schema expectations, return value, and immediate effect of changes, enabling AI agents to understand the operation's impact.

message: Operation 'killSplit' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects, missing_context. Original description uses informal terminology ('kill') without clarifying the actual effect. Enhanced version explains the behavioral consequence (default treatment for all users), clarifies it's not a deletion, specifies immediate effect, and includes return value for better AI agent understanding of this operation's purpose and impact.

data.description_suggestion: Disables (kills) a feature flag in a specific environment, causing all users to receive the default treatment. This operation does not delete the split definition but prevents it from being evaluated. Changes take effect immediately. Returns the updated SplitDefinition object with disabled status. Requires workspace ID, split name, and environment name.
data.reasoning: Original description uses informal terminology ('kill') without clarifying the actual effect. Enhanced version explains the behavioral consequence (default treatment for all users), clarifies it's not a deletion, specifies immediate effect, and includes return value for better AI agent understanding of this operation's purpose and impact.
